issues.objection: A cynical writer's mocking gloss of 7.25 would target either (a) 'just commit to your stack, bro' or (b) 'work harder, but spiritual.' The engineering layer forecloses both: the falsifiability field explicitly rejects the soft reading; the counter_example carves out where the verse does not apply. The quotable_line ("The dashboard is designed for use, not for inspection; the substrate is not concealed by malice but by the structural sh…") is concrete enough to resist parody. The STRETCHED tag is honest for this verse: Verse claims a metaphysical concealment (yoga-māyā-samāvṛtaḥ). Engineering analog cannot inherit the metaphysical claim; operates at the operational scope of dashboards-as-designed-surfaces. Counter-example: senior gatekeeping is engineering pathology, not yoga-māyā at operational scope.

notes: Cynical-writer test for 7.25: identify the parody-handle. The verse does not give one — the engineering layer is anchored to specific tooling and configurations; the quotable line is testable on the artifact, not on affect. PASS.
